|名称 = Sube Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sube Shrine.jpg|250px|Sube Shrine main sanctuaries]]
Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami}} **Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Imperial grandson Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}} *Outer shrine: 69 village shrines of Miyakoda village {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} *Precinct shrines ** Akiba Shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami}} ** Tsushima Shrine / Ubagami Shrine: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すへしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜名区の歴史]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

|名称 = Sube Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sube Shrine.jpg|250px|Sube Shrine main sanctuaries]]
Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami}} **Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Imperial grandson Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}} *Outer shrine: 69 village shrines of Miyakoda village {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} *Precinct shrines ** Akiba Shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami}} ** Tsushima Shrine / Ubagami Shrine: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すへしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜名区の歴史]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

|名称 = Sube Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sube Shrine.jpg|250px|Sube Shrine main sanctuaries]]
Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami}} **Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Imperial grandson Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}} *Outer shrine: 69 village shrines of Miyakoda village {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} *Precinct shrines ** Akiba Shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami}} ** Tsushima Shrine / Ubagami Shrine: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} === Festivals === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すへしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜名区の歴史]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

|名称 = Sube Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sube Shrine.jpg|250px|Sube Shrine main sanctuaries]]
Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010|1=887}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138|1=Inasa District, Shizuoka}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518|1=Emperor Kōkō}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548|1=Miyakodacho}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831|1=mikuriya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū|1=Shinmei shrines}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} **Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Imperial grandson Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} *Outer shrine: 69 village shrines of Miyakoda village {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} *Precinct shrines ** Akiba Shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} ** Tsushima Shrine / Ubagami Shrine: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} === Festivals === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すへしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜名区の歴史]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

|名称 = Sube Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sube Shrine.jpg|250px|Sube Shrine main sanctuaries]]
Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010|1=887}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138|1=Inasa District, Shizuoka}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518|1=Emperor Kōkō}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548|1=Miyakodacho}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831|1=mikuriya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū|1=Shinmei shrines}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} === Festivals === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すへしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜名区の歴史]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010|1=887}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138|1=Inasa District, Shizuoka}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518|1=Emperor Kōkō}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548|1=Miyakodacho}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831|1=mikuriya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū|1=Shinmei shrines}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} **Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Imperial grandson Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} *Outer shrine: 69 village shrines of Miyakoda village {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} *Precinct shrines ** Akiba Shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} ** Tsushima Shrine / Ubagami Shrine: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} === Festivals === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すへしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜名区の歴史]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010|1=887}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138|1=Inasa District, Shizuoka}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518|1=Emperor Kōkō}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548|1=Miyakodacho}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831|1=mikuriya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū|1=Shinmei shrines}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} **Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Imperial grandson Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} *Outer shrine: 69 village shrines of Miyakoda village {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} *Precinct shrines ** Akiba Shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} ** Tsushima Shrine / Ubagami Shrine: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} === Festivals === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:浜名区の歴史]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4080405000864 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県浜松市浜名区都田町6284', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:須倍神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664504}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sube Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sube Shrine.jpg|250px|Sube Shrine main sanctuaries]]
Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010|1=887}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138|1=Inasa District, Shizuoka}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518|1=Emperor Kōkō}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548|1=Miyakodacho}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831|1=mikuriya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū|1=Shinmei shrines}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} **Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Imperial grandson Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} *Outer shrine: 69 village shrines of Miyakoda village {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} *Precinct shrines ** Akiba Shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} ** Tsushima Shrine / Ubagami Shrine: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} === Festivals === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:History of Hamana Ward]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

|名称 = Sube Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sube Shrine.jpg|250px|Sube Shrine main sanctuaries]]
Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010|1=887}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138|1=Inasa District, Shizuoka}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518|1=Emperor Kōkō}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548|1=Miyakodacho}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831|1=mikuriya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū|1=Shinmei shrines}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} **Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Imperial grandson Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} *Outer shrine: 69 village shrines of Miyakoda village {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} *Precinct shrines ** Akiba Shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} ** Tsushima Shrine / Ubagami Shrine: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} === Festivals === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:History of Hamana Ward]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

|名称 = Sube Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sube Shrine.jpg|250px|Sube Shrine main sanctuaries]]
Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010|1=887}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138|1=Inasa District, Shizuoka}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518|1=Emperor Kōkō}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548|1=Miyakodacho}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831|1=mikuriya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū|1=Shinmei shrines}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} **Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Imperial grandson Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} *Outer shrine: 69 village shrines of Miyakoda village {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} *Precinct shrines ** Akiba Shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} ** Tsushima Shrine / Ubagami Shrine: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} === Festivals === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:History of Hamana Ward]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]]

|名称 = Sube Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sube Shrine.jpg|250px|Sube Shrine main sanctuaries]]
Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010|1=887}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138|1=Inasa District, Shizuoka}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518|1=Emperor Kōkō}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548|1=Miyakodacho}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831|1=mikuriya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū|1=Shinmei shrines}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} **Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Imperial grandson Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} *Outer shrine: 69 village shrines of Miyakoda village {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} *Precinct shrines ** Akiba Shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} ** Tsushima Shrine / Ubagami Shrine: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} === Festivals === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:History of Hamana Ward]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * すべじんじゃhttps://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == postal code (P281) == * 431-2102https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4080405000864 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県浜松市浜名区都田町6284', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:須倍神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664504}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sube Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sube Shrine.jpg|250px|Sube Shrine main sanctuaries]]
Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010|1=887}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138|1=Inasa District, Shizuoka}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518|1=Emperor Kōkō}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548|1=Miyakodacho}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831|1=mikuriya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū|1=Shinmei shrines}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} **Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Imperial grandson Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} *Outer shrine: 69 village shrines of Miyakoda village {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} *Precinct shrines ** Akiba Shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} ** Tsushima Shrine / Ubagami Shrine: {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} === Festivals === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:History of Hamana Ward]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4080405000864 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県浜松市浜名区都田町6284', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:須倍神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11664504}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Sube Shrine |画像=[[Image:Sube Shrine.jpg|250px|Sube Shrine main sanctuaries]]
Main sanctuaries
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E}}}} |所在地 ={{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}{{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}} Miyakoda-chō 6284 |位置 ={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|26.7|N|137|42|45.6|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} (Inner Shrine)
{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} (Outer Shrine) |社格 ={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}、former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 ={{ill|lt=887|Q25010|WD=Q25010|1=887}} ({{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3) |本殿 ={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} (two main sanctuaries) |別名 =Shinmei-gū |例祭 =October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Sube Shrine|position=bottom|width=180|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Sube Shrine''' (すべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hamana-ku|Q117077169|WD=Q117077169|1=Hamana-ku, Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Hamamatsu|Q185125|WD=Q185125|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, one of six shrines of {{ill|lt=Inasa District|Q11487138|WD=Q11487138|1=Inasa District, Shizuoka}} in {{ill|lt=Tōtōmi Province|Q1048864|WD=Q1048864|1=Tōtōmi Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3 (887), during the reign of the 58th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōkō|Q440518|WD=Q440518|1=Emperor Kōkō}}. Its location, {{ill|lt=Miyakoda-chō|Q131901548|WD=Q131901548|1=Miyakodacho}}, developed as a {{ill|lt=mikuriya|Q11488831|WD=Q11488831|1=mikuriya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, and the deities are thought to have been invited from Ise. Originally, the Inner Shrine stood as upper shrine on Uenoyama in Kami-Miyakoda Sube, and the Outer Shrine stood as lower shrine in Nakatsu of Shimo-Miyakoda. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 2 (902), both were moved to the present site and came to be called {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine|Q11590310|WD=Q11590310|lt=Shinmei-gū|1=Shinmei shrines}} or Sube Shrine. === Enshrined deities === *Inner Shrine main sanctuary ({{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=chigi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} inward-cut, {{ill|lt=chigi and katsuogi|Q87220234|WD=Q87220234|lt=katsuogi|1=chigi and katsuogi}} six) **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Main deity|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} **{{ill|lt=Enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Associated deities|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|WD=Q11110848|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} *Outer Shrine main sanctuary (chigi outward-cut, katsuogi five) **Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} **Associated deities: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Imperial grandson Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} *Outer shrine: 69 village shrines of Miyakoda village {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} *Precinct shrines ** Akiba Shrine: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} ** Tsushima Shrine / Ubagami Shrine: === At the autumn {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=annual grand festival|1=UNKNOWN}}, floats from nine surrounding districts (Kawayama, Araki, Yokoo, Yoshikage, Tanigami, Isshiki, Nakatsu, Nakano, Sube) are pulled around. Also, at the {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, children selected from duty districts and ujiko participate by serving offering rites and dedicating {{ill|lt=Urayasu no Mai|Q11558194|WD=Q11558194|1=Urayasu no mai}} (sword dance and bell dance). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://katm428.jpn.org/ Sube Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sube Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:浜松市の神社]] [[Category:History of Hamana Ward]] [[Category:浜名区の建築物]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]]
